front: Novel: uses the euphemism 'fug' throughout; set on the fictional Filipino island Anopopei during WWII
back: The Naked and the Dead (Norman Mailer)

front: Novel: Sam Croft crushes a baby bird and kills a POW; orders an attack on Mount Anaka
back: The Naked and the Dead (Norman Mailer)

front: Novel: about the 1967 March on the Pentagon; split into sections titled 'History as a Novel' and 'The Novel as History'
back: The Armies of the Night (Norman Mailer)

front: Novel: Pulitzer-winning account of the execution of Gary Gilmore in Utah, who murders two men and demands his own death penalty
Work: essay describing the hipster as a 'philosophical psychopath' who absorbed black culture; calls jazz 'the music of orgasm'; collected in Advertisements for Myself
Novel: Stephen Rojack murders his estranged wife Deborah; first paragraph mentions Robert Kennedy
Novel: set in ancient Egypt; Menenhetet reincarnated three times; serves as charioteer for Ramses II at Battle of Kadesh
Author: stabbed wife Adele Morales at a party launching his mayoral campaign after being told he wasn't as good as Dostoevsky
Author: feud with Gore Vidal culminated on The Dick Cavett Show; punched Vidal and was told 'once again, words have failed you'
Author: Kate Millett mocked him as 'prisoner of the virility cult' in Sexual Politics; responded with The Prisoner of Sex
Author: wrote The Fight about the 'Rumble in the Jungle'; bit off a chunk of Rip Torn's ear on the set of Maidstone
